prerequisites: A Dwolla account with access to the Dwolla Dashboard (use the Sandbox Dashboard for testing or the production Dashboard for live data).
An application registered in the Dwolla Dashboard, which provides the key (client_id) and secret (client_secret).
Decide which environment to connect to, since the environment config field selects between Dwolla's Sandbox and Production APIs.
A start_date to bound the earliest records the source reads.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 client credentials (key and secret)
authMethods.summary: Dwolla authorizes API requests using the OAuth 2.0 client credentials grant. The connector's client_id (key) and client_secret identify your registered application. They are sent in an HTTP Basic Authorization header to the /token endpoint with grant_type=client_credentials to obtain a short-lived bearer access token (default 3600 seconds, no refresh token), which is then passed as Authorization: Bearer {access_token} on each request.

setupSteps.title: Register an application in the Dwolla Dashboard
setupSteps.body: Create an application in the Dwolla Dashboard to obtain its credentials. Dwolla provides separate Sandbox and Production environments, so create the application in the dashboard matching the environment you intend to sync. The application's key becomes the connector's client_id and the secret becomes the client_secret.

setupSteps.title: Provide the key and secret
setupSteps.body: Enter the application's key as client_id and its secret as client_secret. These are the credentials Dwolla exchanges via the client credentials grant for an access token, so keep the secret confidential and never expose it client-side.

setupSteps.title: Select the environment
setupSteps.body: Set the required environment config field to match where your application and credentials were created. Sandbox targets Dwolla's testing API (https://api-sandbox.dwolla.com) and Production targets the live API (https://api.dwolla.com). The credentials are environment-specific and are not interchangeable between Sandbox and Production.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date config field to define the earliest point from which the connector should read data.
